mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer infrastructure as code or GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and potential complexity in multi-instance setups.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm with a specific version.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with specified configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Verifies the Grafana instance and its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the Grafana instance.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana instances. After upgrading, verify that the instances are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator.
> Verifies the Grafana instance after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating Grafana instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD for Grafana is not installed or not available.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana CRD is installed using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/grafana/grafana-operator/main/deploy/crds/grafana.integreatly.org_grafanas_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana instance is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to incorrect configuration in the Grafana CR.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana pod using 'kubectl logs <grafana-pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard not loading
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the GrafanaDashboard CR is not correctly defined or applied.

**Fix:**
Verify the GrafanaDashboard CR definition and reapply it using 'kubectl apply -f <dashboard-file.yaml>'.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm repository is not correctly added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts' and 'helm repo update'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Grafana v9.5, improved CRD validation, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the command 'kubectl get grafana grafana' returning a status of 'Running'.

kind: Grafana
metadata:

  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
